No Poverty.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule directly links MIS to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no mention of food security, nutrition, sustainable agriculture, or related topics in the course materials.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not address health services, disease prevention, well-being, or related health targets.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and aims to cultivate skills for IT entrepreneurship and employment (Target 4.4), directly aligning with SDG 4.
evidence: cultivate a deeper understanding of information systems and emerging technologies

Gender Equality.reason: While 'Ethical & Social Issues in MIS' could potentially touch upon gender, there's no explicit evidence it addresses gender equality or women's empowerment through ICT (Target 5.b). The link is very weak.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to water quality, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course does not cover energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly focuses on 'business model innovation,' 'IT entrepreneurship,' and 'competitive advantage,' directly supporting technological upgrading and innovation for economic productivity (Target 8.2) and entrepreneurship (Target 8.3).
evidence: business model innovation and information technology (IT) entrepreneurship

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamentally about 'IT Infrastructure & Emerging Technologies,' 'business model innovation,' and 'information system applications,' directly addressing technological capabilities, innovation, and ICT access (Targets 9.1, 9.4, 9.5, 9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course welcomes 'exchange students' and addresses 'Ethical & Social Issues in MIS,' inferring a contribution to inclusion and potentially reducing inequalities in access to education and technology (Target 10.2).
evidence: We extremely welcome exchange students to join us!

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No course content relates to urban planning, housing, transport, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B explicitly mentions 'sustainability issues.' Topics like 'Enterprise Applications' and 'sustainable competitive advantage' can support resource efficiency and sustainable practices (Targets 12.2, 12.6).
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific focus on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or related policies.

Life Below Water.reason: The course does not address marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence suggests the course covers terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, forests, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Topics like 'IT Security' and 'Ethical & Social Issues in MIS' contribute to developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions by ensuring data integrity and ethical conduct (Targets 16.5, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course covers 'Global IS Management' and encourages 'cooperation with our local students' for exchange students, fostering knowledge sharing and international cooperation through enabling technology (Targets 17.6, 17.8).
evidence: cooperation with our local students will benefit your learning experience in Taiwan
